description: Existing-code bug audit family: 4 skills (audit-planner / audit-reviewer / audit-verifier / audit-reporter) + 2 agents (reviewer/verifier roles for two-stage confirmation). Before slicing, audit-planner detects the project's language + architecture (e.g. C/C++ embedded SOA, Python web service, frontend SPA) and proposes a tailored review checklist (scenario preset bug categories) for the user to confirm. Then slices the repository into small directory-tree modules with tight per-module budgets (default token=12000 / files=8 since v0.3.0) and reviewer scans ONE module per fresh session to avoid context compression — independent contexts both across reviewer<>verifier and across modules within reviewer. Drafts findings constrained to the confirmed checklist with evidence (file, line, category, severity, reasoning, code snippet, suggested fix), then runs an independent verifier agent to confirm/reject/upgrade/downgrade each finding with its own evidence_check. Emits a single-file HTML report (always; renders the project profile + checklist in the header) and an optional Excel workbook. Distinct from packs/coding/hf-code-review (PR diff review, single agent, no report).
